Enterprise AI · Data · CRM/Salesforce · Workflow · Regulated Change

Enterprise AI has entered the execution phase.

Chandra Kanojia helps large regulated enterprises make AI useful inside real work: trusted data, CRM/Salesforce, workflow, analytics, service platforms, controls, and operating routines.

The demo is easy. The data and work are hard.

View Executive Profile Read Enterprise AI Point of View

Operating proof spans

Regulated financial services · data readiness for AI · Salesforce/CRM modernization · enterprise workflow · service and advisor platforms · analytics and governance · platform reliability · insurance, retirement, brokerage, healthcare, claims, and global consulting transformation.

Where Chandra creates leverage

The work has to change before AI matters.

01

Make AI useful beyond the demo

Move AI from pilots into real workflows with clear ownership, evidence, controls, adoption paths, and business accountability.

02

Modernize the systems where work moves

Improve CRM, case management, analytics, service platforms, and workflow systems so they guide action instead of only storing activity.

03

Turn data into decision context

Make trusted data available at the point of work, where people, systems, and AI have to decide what happens next.

Executive briefings

A practical conversation for boards and leadership teams.

The useful AI conversation has moved past demos. Boards and executive teams now need to ask whether the enterprise can change the work, trust the data, control the risk, and see the value.

View Briefing Topics

AI after the demo

What changes when AI moves from assistance to execution.

The data problem under AI strategy

Why trusted context at the point of work matters more than another dashboard.

CRM as a system of action

Why the next CRM advantage is better decisions and follow-through, not more data capture.

Field Notes

What large enterprises learn after the AI demo.

View Field Notes

Enterprise AI

AI Is Moving Faster Than the Enterprise.

The technology is moving quickly. The harder question is whether the enterprise can change work, governance, data, and decision-making fast enough to use it well.

Data

The Data Problem Hiding Under Every AI Strategy.

Most AI plans talk about models. The harder question is whether trusted context reaches the workflow when a decision has to happen.

Discuss the mandate

The useful conversation starts after the demo.

Talk to Chandra about making AI work inside regulated environments: trusted data, CRM/Salesforce, workflow, controls, adoption, and measurable business value.
